prompt: Describe the regulatory framework governing how hospital formularies are developed, including the role of pharmacy and therapeutics committees in evaluating medication options and establishing drug selection protocols.

Describe the standard diagnostic workup for prostate cancer, including PSA testing, biopsy procedures, Gleason scoring systems, and how staging determines treatment options such as active surveillance, surgery, radiation, or hormone therapy.

Describe the technical principles underlying electroencephalography (EEG) and positron emission tomography (PET) scans, including how each modality detects neural activity and what types of neurological information each provides about brain function and structure.
Describe the pathophysiology of Parkinson's disease, including the role of dopaminergic neuron loss in the substantia nigra and current pharmacological approaches to managing motor symptoms.

Explain the pathophysiology of chronic diseases such as type 2 diabetes mellitus and coronary artery disease, including how insulin resistance develops and how atherosclerotic plaques form in blood vessels.
Describe the biochemical principles underlying glucose oxidase-based blood glucose measurement and how electrochemical sensors detect glucose concentration in whole blood samples.
Describe the standard medical consultation process, including how physicians request input from specialists and what information flows occur during peer-to-peer clinical discussions.

Describe the technical process of speech-to-text transcription in clinical settings, including acoustic modeling, language modeling, and how speaker diarization separates multiple voices in audio recordings.

Describe the anatomical distribution of adipose tissue in humans, including subcutaneous versus visceral fat depots, and how body composition varies across populations due to genetic and environmental factors.

Describe the diagnostic criteria for epilepsy according to the International League Against Epilepsy (ILAE), including what constitutes a seizure, how many unprovoked seizures are required for diagnosis, and what role EEG and neuroimaging play in the diagnostic workup.
Describe the pharmacological mechanisms by which dopamine agonists and levodopa formulations modulate basal ganglia circuitry in Parkinson's disease treatment.

Explain the technical principles behind photoplethysmography (PPG) sensors in smartphones, including how they detect changes in blood volume and the signal processing algorithms used to extract heart rate from raw optical data.
Describe the diagnostic criteria and pathophysiology of endometriosis and fibromyalgia, including how these conditions are identified through clinical examination, patient history, and biomarkers.
Explain the biological mechanisms of organ rejection and how the major histocompatibility complex (MHC) determines transplant compatibility between donors and recipients.

Describe the mechanisms by which robotic surgical systems translate surgeon hand movements into instrument motion, including the role of haptic feedback, degrees of freedom, and tremor filtration in modern surgical platforms.

Explain the FDA drug approval process, including how Phase III clinical trials establish efficacy for specific indications and what pharmacological criteria determine a drug's approved therapeutic uses.

Describe the anatomical changes involved in Roux-en-Y gastric bypass surgery, including the creation of the gastric pouch, the biliopancreatic limb, and the alimentary limb, and explain how these modifications alter nutrient absorption and gastric emptying.

Describe the documentation requirements for chemotherapy administration in clinical oncology, including what information must be recorded about drug dosages, timing, and patient monitoring parameters.
Describe the clinical indications for colonoscopy screening, including age-based guidelines and risk stratification criteria used to determine screening intervals for colorectal cancer detection.

Describe the typical workflow and time constraints of healthcare professionals in clinical settings, including administrative duties, patient care responsibilities, and continuing education requirements.
Describe the regulatory framework governing health claims in advertising, including the distinction between structure-function claims and disease claims under FDA guidelines.

Describe the economic models used in healthcare delivery systems to determine resource allocation between preventive services, acute care, and chronic disease management in different insurance structures.

Describe the sensitivity and specificity of common cancer screening modalities such as mammography, colonoscopy, and low-dose CT for lung cancer, and explain how lead time bias and overdiagnosis affect the interpretation of screening efficacy data.
Describe the regulatory framework for pharmaceutical labeling, including how efficacy data and adverse event frequencies are derived from clinical trial protocols and reported in package inserts.

Describe the legal framework governing informed consent for medical procedures on minors, including the concepts of parental authority, mature minor doctrine, and emancipated minor status across different jurisdictions.

Describe the regulatory framework governing informed consent in clinical research, including the key elements required by institutional review boards and the distinction between therapeutic and non-therapeutic procedures.
Describe the psychological and physiological mechanisms underlying chronic illness experience, including how persistent symptoms affect patient perception and communication with healthcare providers.
Describe the physiological mechanisms of chronic pain, including peripheral and central sensitization, and how nociceptive pathways differ from neuropathic pain signals in the nervous system.
Describe the procedural coding system used in Medicare billing, including how CPT codes are structured and how they map to reimbursement rates in the fee-for-service model.

Describe the structure and function of physician credentialing databases, including what types of professional information are typically recorded and how healthcare organizations use credentialing data for provider directories.

Describe the standard Emergency Severity Index (ESI) triage system used in emergency departments, including how it categorizes patients into levels based on acuity and anticipated resource consumption.

Describe the diagnostic criteria and clinical assessment methods psychiatrists use to evaluate mental health status, including standardized instruments like structured clinical interviews and the DSM-5 diagnostic framework.
Describe the pathophysiology of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), including the roles of emphysema and chronic bronchitis in airflow limitation and gas exchange impairment.

Describe the physiological process of blood transfusion, including how blood type compatibility is determined, the role of antigens and antibodies in transfusion reactions, and the typical flow rates and monitoring parameters used during transfusion administration.
Describe the mechanisms by which clinical practice guidelines for cardiovascular disease are developed, disseminated, and incorporated into standard treatment protocols.

Describe the neuropathological hallmarks of Alzheimer's disease, including amyloid-beta plaques and neurofibrillary tangles, and explain how these changes correlate with cognitive decline across disease stages.
Describe the organizational structure of public health campaigns, including how health departments coordinate multi-stakeholder initiatives and allocate resources across prevention programs.

Explain the scientific principles behind drug testing in competitive sports, including the detection windows for different substance classes and how mass spectrometry identifies banned compounds in biological samples.

Describe the standard diagnostic pathway for cancer treatment initiation, including the role of histopathological confirmation, molecular profiling, and staging procedures in determining appropriate chemotherapy regimens.

Describe the technical principles of facial reconstruction surgery, including how surgeons use tissue grafts, bone structure analysis, and soft tissue manipulation to restore facial anatomy after trauma or injury.
Describe the technical architecture of electronic health record (EHR) systems, including how data is stored, version-controlled, and accessed through HL7 or FHIR standards.
Describe the operational workflow of patient registration systems in healthcare facilities, including how scheduling software integrates with billing codes and insurance verification processes.

Describe the pharmacological principles underlying intramuscular versus subcutaneous injection, including tissue absorption rates, depot formation, and how different needle gauges and injection depths affect drug bioavailability.

Describe the regulatory framework governing pharmaceutical sample distribution in the United States, including DEA scheduling classifications and the Prescription Drug Marketing Act requirements for sample tracking and disposal.

Describe the pharmacological mechanism of action of opioid analgesics, including their binding to mu, delta, and kappa opioid receptors, and explain how the WHO analgesic ladder guides stepwise pain management in clinical practice.
Describe the pathophysiology of chronic disease progression, including how conditions like diabetes, hypertension, and COPD typically advance through defined clinical stages over time.

Describe the diagnostic criteria in DSM-5 for major psychiatric disorders and explain how differential diagnosis is performed in clinical psychiatry to distinguish between overlapping symptom presentations.

Describe the diagnostic criteria for multiple sclerosis, including McDonald criteria, MRI findings such as demyelinating lesions in space and time, and the role of cerebrospinal fluid analysis in confirming CNS inflammation.
Describe the pathophysiology of acne vulgaris, including the role of sebaceous glands, Propionibacterium acnes colonization, and inflammatory mediators in lesion formation.

Describe the pathophysiology of acute coronary syndrome, including how myocardial ischemia presents clinically and what diagnostic criteria are used to differentiate stable angina from unstable angina and myocardial infarction.

Describe the regulatory framework governing pharmaceutical marketing to physicians, including FDA guidelines on promotional activities and the role of clinical practice guidelines in evidence-based prescribing.

Describe the typical workflow and data fields involved in patient intake systems at medical practices, including insurance verification, appointment scheduling algorithms, and queue management protocols.

Describe the structural components of modern surgical facilities, including operating room design standards, sterile field requirements, and the essential equipment categories needed for different types of surgical procedures.

Describe the pharmaceutical supply chain from manufacturer to patient, including the role of pharmacy benefit managers, formulary design, and how drug pricing is negotiated between manufacturers and insurers.

Describe the mechanism of action of statins in inhibiting HMG-CoA reductase and explain the most common adverse effects associated with statin therapy, including their incidence rates and underlying biological mechanisms.

Describe the epidemiology of melanoma and non-melanoma skin cancers, including geographic variation in incidence rates and the relationship between cumulative UV radiation exposure and skin cancer risk.

Describe the pathophysiology of glucose homeostasis, including the roles of insulin and glucagon in regulating blood glucose levels, and explain how different classes of antidiabetic medications (sulfonylureas, metformin, GLP-1 agonists, SGLT2 inhibitors) mechanistically lower blood glucose.

Explain the pathophysiology of chronic bronchitis, including how particulate matter and gaseous pollutants interact with bronchial epithelium and contribute to airway inflammation and mucus hypersecretion.
Describe the genetic basis of hemoglobinopathies such as sickle cell anemia and thalassemia, including the molecular mutations involved and their geographic distribution patterns.
Describe the physiological mechanisms by which insulin regulates glucose metabolism in Type 1 Diabetes, including the role of ketone body production during periods of reduced carbohydrate intake.

Describe the physiological mechanisms by which macronutrient composition affects metabolic pathways, including insulin signaling, lipid metabolism, and mitochondrial energy production in various tissues.
Describe the clinical criteria and assessment tools used to determine medical stability for hospital discharge, including vital sign parameters, laboratory values, and functional status indicators.
Describe the physiological changes in bone density and microarchitecture that occur with aging and osteoporosis, including how trabecular and cortical bone remodeling rates change over time.

Describe the surgical techniques used in gender affirmation procedures, including vaginoplasty, phalloplasty, and metoidioplasty, and explain the anatomical considerations surgeons must address in these reconstructive operations.

Describe the clinical and epidemiological factors that influence treatment efficacy across different age groups, including how pharmacokinetics, comorbidity profiles, and physiological reserve vary with patient age.
Describe the technical architecture of clinical trial management systems, including how they handle participant registration, data validation, and maintain audit trails for enrollment records.

Describe the diagnostic criteria and clinical assessment procedures used by healthcare professionals to evaluate nutritional deficiencies, including laboratory biomarkers, physical examination findings, and patient history components that inform supplement recommendations.
Explain the physiological criteria used to determine brain death and the medical procedures involved in organ procurement after circulatory or neurological death.
Describe the regulatory pathway by which novel therapeutics are evaluated by agencies like the FDA, including the phases of clinical trials and the criteria used to assess safety and efficacy.
Describe the pharmacological mechanisms of common general anesthetic agents, including how they modulate GABA_A receptors and NMDA receptors to produce unconsciousness.
Describe the mechanism of action of opioid analgesics at the mu, delta, and kappa receptors, including how agonist binding affects neuronal membrane potential and neurotransmitter release.

Describe the physiological mechanisms of irritable bowel syndrome, including visceral hypersensitivity, gut-brain axis dysfunction, and how different macronutrients affect gastrointestinal motility and fermentation patterns in the colon.
Describe the endocannabinoid system's role in pain modulation, including how CB1 and CB2 receptors are distributed in the nervous system and what physiological effects cannabinoid agonists produce.
Describe the economic structure of healthcare service bundling and how pricing models differ between fee-for-service, capitation, and value-based reimbursement systems.

Describe the medical criteria used to evaluate candidates for liver transplantation, including MELD score calculation, contraindications, and the allocation system used by organ procurement organizations.

Describe the economics of healthcare scheduling systems, including how appointment slot allocation, patient flow optimization, and reimbursement structures affect clinic capacity planning and revenue cycles.
Describe the peer review process in academic publishing, including how reviewers evaluate methodology, statistical analysis, and interpretation of results in scientific manuscripts.
Describe the standard clinical trial enrollment criteria for multiple sclerosis treatments, including how eligibility is determined based on disease stage, EDSS scores, and prior treatment history.

Describe the mechanisms of action for currently FDA-approved treatments for amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), including riluzole, edaravone, and the combination drug sodium phenylbutyrate-taurursodiol, and explain how each targets different aspects of motor neuron degeneration.
